title: service-accounts — declared entitlements
description: Five entitlement keys covering the SA capability surface a tenant purchases. Every tenant gets tenant-scoped SAs on any plan (a webhook signer is baseline). Platform-plane SAs are medium+ (cross-tenant integrations imply a paid engagement). IP allowlisting + extended TTL are enterprise controls. Entitlements gate CREATION of an SA carrying the corresponding property; already-issued SAs are grandfathered until rotation.

entitlements.description: SAs bound to a specific tenant (via non-null `tenant_id`). Baseline across every plan tier — webhook signers + tenant-scoped API integrations require this.

SAs without tenant scope (`tenant_id IS NULL`). These are cross-tenant machine credentials — shared observability collectors, cross-tenant sync workers, fleet health probes. Free tier gated off because the use case (multi-tenant integration) doesn't apply to single-tenant customers.

Per-SA IP allowlist stored in metadata. When present, `service_account.audit` middleware refuses requests from non-allowlisted source IPs (403 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_IP_NOT_ALLOWED). Enterprise-tier feature; deferred Wave 1b implementation — the config flag exists in Wave 1a but is not enforced until Wave 1b's audit-middleware upgrade.

Ability to request JWT TTLs beyond the module-default `max_ttl_seconds` (default 3600s). Enterprise-only. Extending TTL beyond one hour trades security posture for integration latency — gated to plans with a matched compliance obligation.

DetectDormantServiceAccountJob dispatches ServiceAccountDormantNotification for the tenant's SAs. Free tier gated off because the notification recipient (tenant's Application owner) doesn't exist on free tier's simpler ops posture.

notes: Entitlements gate CREATION + certain runtime paths (IP allowlist enforcement, TTL bounds). They do NOT gate READ — platform-admin surface always sees every SA. Downgrading a tenant plan does not auto-revoke existing SAs; ops must manually rotate + delete via the platform-admin surface.

The `service_accounts_platform_plane` entitlement is INDEPENDENT of `platform.tenant_admin` (on the PlatformUser blueprint). Both are required for a platform_admin to provision a platform-plane SA — the entitlement gates the CAPABILITY, the platform-admin permission gates the ACTOR.
